description_long: This is a World War II-era military bomber aircraft with a distinctive camouflage paint scheme of tan and green over a black underbelly. The airplane features four large propeller engines mounted on a straight, mid-mounted wing, each engine equipped with a four-blade propeller. The fuselage is long and cylindrical, with a glazed nose section for the bombardier and a multi-windowed cockpit positioned at the front. The tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and a horizontal stabilizer, both camouflaged. The wings display roundels and insignia typical of the Royal Air Force, and the aircraft is equipped with multiple gun turrets for defense. The landing gear is not visible, suggesting it may be retractable.
description: A camouflaged, four-engine World War II bomber with mid-mounted straight wings, RAF insignia, and a glazed nose and cockpit. The aircraft features multiple gun turrets and a single vertical tail fin.
description_view_0: Side profile showing the full length of the fuselage, four engines, RAF roundels on the wings, and the tail fin with insignia.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the straight, mid-mounted wings, camouflage pattern, and the full span of the horizontal stabilizer.
description_view_2: Opposite side profile, displaying the same features as view 0 but with different lighting and visibility of the aircraft code markings.
description_view_3: Frontal view showing the four propeller engines, glazed cockpit and nose, and the symmetry of the wings and tail.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing, straight wings with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Four piston engines, nacelle-mounted on the wings.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Retractable landing gear, likely tricycle configuration, not visible in images.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed multi-seat cockpit with multiple windows and a glazed nose section.
